visual_findings: The round-16 contact sheet showed stable figure/table placement and no hard crop; the selected target was the page-10 Ethical Considerations block because it is reviewer-facing and visibly adjacent to the start of References.

The original ethics text preserved the right claim boundary but read as a compliance checklist and used a mechanical disclosure ending around quantitative claims, citations, reported artifacts, and empirical panels.
The first rewrite improved the disclosure boundary but introduced a loose full-justified line around the human-author responsibility sentence.
The accepted render keeps Ethical Considerations on page 10, starts References cleanly in the right column, and avoids the loose disclosure line from the intermediate render.
The full after contact sheet keeps the 11-page structure stable and does not visibly displace the references, figure stack, tables, Discussion, Conclusion, or Limitations.
change_summary: Reframed ethical risks as indirect synthetic-scene risks: bias, license restrictions, unsafe deployment assumptions, and inflated robustness claims.
Changed the reuse guidance from an ACL-submission checklist to a general submission responsibility for provenance, licensing, filtering, and intended render use.
Added a concise author-responsibility disclosure for AI-assisted implementation, editing, audit drafting, and schematic generation.
Preserved the key boundary that the generated schematic is illustrative only and that quantitative results and empirical visual panels were checked against repository artifacts and cited sources.

verification.command: rg -n "Overfull|Float too large|too large|LaTeX Warning: Float|Rerun to get|Warning:.*Label|Undefined references|multiply defined|Package lineno Warning" paper/venues/acl27/build/main.log || true

residual_risk: Focused Ethical Considerations and disclosure story polish only; this is not a full upload audit, target-policy refresh, or integrity-fingerprint refresh.
